violations.claim: console.log(`TITLE: ${issue.title}`) claims the title is meaningful.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; LINE: 141
; console.log(`TITLE: ${issue.title}`) claims the title is meaningful.
; IssueData.title is typed as string with no non-empty precondition enforced.
; An empty title produces the degenerate output "TITLE: ".
(declare-const title_len Int)
(declare-const log_chars_beyond_prefix Int)
; Transition: log output length = len("TITLE: ") + title_len
(assert (= title_len 0))
(assert (= log_chars_beyond_prefix title_len))  ; template literal appends title
; Violation: log implies meaningful title, but title_len = 0 is reachable
(assert (= log_chars_beyond_prefix 0))
(check-sat)
; sat → title="" passes TypeScript types; dry-run prints "TITLE: " with no content
